channel.title: Tooling on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Tooling on emsenn.net

item.title: GnoponEmacs Overview
item.description:
GnoponEmacs is the Emacs environment for interacting with the Emsemioverse.

Purpose

  • Provide a consistent editing environment.
  • Support semiotic workflows across markdown, skills, and study materials.
  • Serve as the primary TUI/GUI for agents and humans.

item.title: GnoponEmacs Setup
item.description:
This lesson outlines the setup steps for GnoponEmacs in this repository.

Setup steps

  • Use the repository init.el as the base configuration.
  • Keep settings in version control and avoid local-only divergences when possible.
  • Document any new packages or keybindings in the GnoponEmacs README.

item.title: Reworking GnoponEmacs as a RITM tool
item.description:
When considering GnoponEmacs as a research tool for Relational Infinity-Topos Modality, I want it to be what lets me, essentially, simulate coKleisli action on my research archive as a relational topology.

Starting at what’s immutable and unique: the :ID: property every Org-roam node has.

We’re going to start with one node, called “node” representing what a node at its most general is in this modality, and so it will have a property, :node-relations:, that works like the following:

item.title: Hive Auto Ledger
item.description:
Hive Auto Ledger

Last updated from triage notes on 2025-08-26.

Overview

Hive Auto-Ledger (HAL) is a speculative platform designed to manage Hive blockchain wallets with a focus on transparency, usability, and automation. The app will provide tools to track wallet contents, manage transactions, and analyze market data, all while maintaining an accurate Beancount ledger. Future iterations will integrate advanced features such as trade bots, post analytics, CMS capabilities, and tokenomics.

item.title: Semiotic Markdown
item.description:
Semiotic Markdown Specification

This specification describes how Markdown files, as ingested by the semiotic publisher [@semiotic-publisher], are interpreted as concepts in the Semiotic Universe [@semiotic-universe] and its Stewardable Semiotic Concept Universe extension [@stewardable-semiotic-concept-universe], and how their structure is mapped into atoms, fragments, and publication views.

Normative statements in this document use “MUST”, “SHOULD”, and “MAY” in the conventional requirements-language sense.

1. File Model

  • Throughout this specification, the loader denotes the Markdown‑to‑concept stage of the semiotic publisher [@semiotic-publisher].
